title: Attention Feed Phase 3a: Build the digest engine that turns feed noise into token-efficient deltas
description:
# Why this bead exists
A frontier LLM does not usually want the raw event stream first. Most of the time it wants a compact answer to "what changed that I should care about?" This bead builds the digest engine that turns feed noise into token-efficient deltas while preserving the ability to drill into raw events when needed.

# Scope
1. Coalesce recent feed activity into a compact digest grouped by `actionable`, `interesting`, `background`, and suppressed counts.
2. Generate a stable summary string that is cheap for an operator LLM to consume.
3. Preserve cursor boundaries so the digest can be chained cleanly in a long-running tending loop.
4. Retain event references/details needed for targeted follow-up rather than forcing a fresh snapshot.
5. Avoid aggressive magic. Coalescing should be rule-based and testable, not mysterious.
6. Establish explicit suppression rules for repetitive output churn, lifecycle noise, and redundant alert spam so later changes do not quietly make digests unusably noisy.

# Design considerations
- Repeated passive output should usually be suppressed, not surfaced one-by-one.
- Important coordination or stall signals must survive coalescing.
- The digest should remain predictable enough that agents can build habits around it.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Tests for suppression of noisy output bursts.
- Tests that important signals survive coalescing.
- Golden tests for summary stability and bucket assignment.
- Benchmarks or stress tests for digesting large bursts without pathological allocations.
- Debug logging in tests that shows which source events were surfaced, coalesced, or suppressed and why.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The digest separates action-required changes from passive background churn.
- [ ] Summary strings remain compact but concrete.
- [ ] Suppression/coalescing rules are deterministic and testable.
- [ ] Cursor boundaries remain explicit.
- [ ] Digest noise-control behavior is diagnosable from test output.

dependencies.title: Attention Feed Phase 1c: Derive the first-class attention signals an operator agent actually cares about

dependents.title: Attention Feed Phase 3b: Ship --robot-digest as the default what-changed surface
